     The Numbers Business Report

The Huntsman: Winter’s War (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $48,003,015Details
International Box Office $117,146,287
Worldwide Box Office $165,149,302
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,950,887
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,440,918
Total $15,391,805
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Evil Queen Ravenna betrays her good sister with an unforgivable act, freezing Freya’s heart to love and unleashing in her an icy power she never knew she possessed. Retreating to a kingdom far to the north, Freya raises an army of Huntsmen as her protectors, with the only rule that no two of them should ever fall in love. As a war for domination escalates between the two queens, the hero standing between them is Freya’s most elite Huntsman, Eric. Alongside fellow warrior Sara—the only woman who has ever captured his heart—Eric must help Freya vanquish her sister…or Ravenna’s wickedness will rule for eternity.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,445,035 (40.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$115,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,791 opening theaters/3,802 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $66,371,805

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 22nd, 2016 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: April 6th, 2016 (Wide), released as The Huntsman: Winter's War (Italy)
April 6th, 2016 (Wide), released as The Huntsman: Winter's War (Thailand)
April 7th, 2016 (Wide), released as The Huntsman: Winter's War (Austria)
April 7th, 2016 (Wide), released as The Huntsman: Winter's War (Denmark)
April 7th, 2016 (Wide), released as The Huntsman: Winter's War (Germany)
Video Release: August 2nd, 2016 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for fantasy action violence and some sensuality.
(Rating bulletin 2414 (Cert #50275), 3/2/2016)
Running Time: 113 minutes
Franchise: Snow White and the Huntsman
Cast, crew, or production detail: Motion Capture Performance, Prequel, Sequels Without Their Original Stars
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion
Filming location (country): England, United Kingdom
Filming location (locale): Puzzlewood, Gloucestershire, England
Filming location (state or county): Gloucestershire, England
Plot point: Death of a Sibling, Dwarves, Royalty
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Folk Tale/Legend/Fairytale
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, perfect world (Beijing) Pictures Co, Roth Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Evil Queen Ravenna betrays her good sister with an unforgivable act, freezing Freya’s heart to love and unleashing in her an icy power she never knew she possessed. Retreating to a kingdom far to the north, Freya raises an army of Huntsmen as her protectors, with the only rule that no two of them should ever fall in love. As a war for domination escalates between the two queens, the hero standing between them is Freya’s most elite Huntsman, Eric. Alongside fellow warrior Sara—the only woman who has ever captured his heart—Eric must help Freya vanquish her sister…or Ravenna’s wickedness will rule for eternity.
